title: How to apply to the Horizon Shortfall Scheme Appeals (HSSA) process
body: "Timelines for opening  HSSA is open to applications from all postmasters who are eligible to apply as outlined in the eligibility criteria.  An independent HSS appeals process   At the time of HSSA opening to appeals, over 5,000 claimants have settled their Horizon Shortfall Scheme (HSS) claim, and many will be satisfied with the outcome. However, some individuals may not have been aware that they could claim for certain losses or they did not receive legal support to dispute their offer.  The government agrees that it is appropriate to implement the Horizon Compensation Advisory Board’s June 2023 recommendation that an independent appeals process should be introduced for HSS. As advised by the board, HSSA’s focus is “assessing whether settlements were fair based on the evidence provided, while allowing consideration of elements of a claim which had been missed or not included on the original form”.   DBT oversees the appeals process with the independent Horizon Compensation Advisory Board providing advice on its delivery. Although we request the disclosure of information relating to your HSS case from Post Office, they are not involved in any assessment of your appeal.   The board is made up of:   2 academic experts in the field of alternative dispute resolution and legal ethics  2 parliamentarians recognised for their past involvement in pursuing the resolution of the Horizon scandal   Who can make an appeal under HSSA   You are eligible for HSSA if you were eligible for HSS and meet one of the following criteria:    you have settled your claim in the HSS without entering the Dispute Resolution Process (DRP)  you have rejected your initial HSS offer without entering the DRP  you have settled your claim in the DRP before mediation stage without legal advice funded by Post Office, other than for reasonable allowances to consider your offer you are within the DRP (with or without legal advice) but have not requested or be awaiting a mediation meeting at the time of HSSA opening   To help postmasters and their representatives decide whether DRP or HSSA would be the best dispute process for their individual circumstances, read more about the main differences between the 2 processes.   HSS claimants who have accepted the £75,000 fixed sum payment   On 9 October 2025, the government announced that those who accepted the £75,000 Fixed Sum Offer (FSO) will be able to seek permission to appeal to HSSA.  This process is not available yet and you should not yet apply to HSSA if you have accepted the FSO. Details on the permission to appeal criteria will be published on this page once the process opens.   HSS claimants who decline the £75,000 fixed sum payment but get a lower HSSA offer   In the HSS, you can accept the £75,000 fixed sum payment either upfront or within 3 months of receiving your first fully assessed offer. If the 3 month period has passed, you cannot later accept the £75,000 fixed sum.  If you enter HSSA, you will lose any right you may have to accept the FSO. This...
